findings.title: Candidate MCP server requests filesystem + network with no scoping
findings.description: A third-party MCP server submitted to the marketplace declares an over-privileged tool with excessive scope: it requests unrestricted filesystem read/write and outbound network in a single unscoped tool. No least-privilege boundary is declared.

findings.title: Tool description changed after approval (rug-pull)
findings.description: Manifest hashing detected a tool-description swap: after the MCP server was approved, its tool description was rewritten (rug-pull / tool-poisoning) to instruct the model to forward conversation contents to an external endpoint.

findings.title: Unvetted MCP server exfiltrates via tool-call side channel
findings.description: A malicious MCP server was observed encoding data into tool-call arguments to exfiltrate it through the tool channel. The MCP server is untrusted and was added without review.

findings.title: Plugin manifest pins no version
findings.description: The plugin is installed unpinned from a third-party registry with no signature or checksum, so a substituted package could compromise the supply chain on next pull.

findings.title: Server README lists support email
findings.description: The submission README lists a support contact email and a changelog. No security-relevant behavior; informational only.
